Which industries get the most Saskatchewan funding?

Agriculture & Agri-Food

Food processing, pulse crops, specialty grains, and AgTech

Mining & Resources

Potash, uranium, oil services, and mining technology

Technology

AgTech, clean technology, software, and digital innovation

Clean Energy

Renewable energy, carbon capture, and sustainability

Life Sciences

Vaccine development, animal health, and biopharmaceuticals

Indigenous Business

Specialized programs for Indigenous entrepreneurs

shortAnswer: Yes — Saskatchewan small businesses can access Innovation Saskatchewan grants (SAIF/AGF up to $400K), investor tax credits (STSI offering 30% to angels), and the Canada-Saskatchewan Job Grant (reimbursing up to 66% / $10,000 per trainee). These stack with federal programs like IRAP and SR&ED.
shortAnswerQuestion: What small business grants and funding are available in Saskatchewan for 2026?

faq.question: What is the Saskatchewan Technology Startup Incentive (STSI) limit?
faq.answer: STSI offers a 30% non-refundable tax credit to investors who back qualified early-stage technology startups in the province. Startups must apply to be certified before raising funds.

faq.question: Are SAIF and AGF grants repayable?
faq.answer: No. Innovation Saskatchewan SAIF and Ag-Tech Growth Fund (AGF) are structured as non-repayable grants, though they require milestone progress reporting.

faq.question: Does the government fund crop growing directly?
faq.answer: Standard crop production does not qualify for tech grants. However, value-added food processing equipment upgrades qualify under the SAVI program for up to 50% matching.

expertTip.title: Emphasize Agriculture-adjacent Tech
expertTip.content: Saskatchewan economic programs heavily prioritize value-added agriculture. Even software and IoT startups can double their chances of funding by highlighting applications for crop storage, logistics, or food processing.

seo.metaTitle: Saskatchewan Small Business Grants 2026: Complete SME Guide
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to qualify and apply for Saskatchewan Small Business Grants in 2026. Access active SAIF tech grants, STSI investor tax credits, and SAVI agricultural matching.
